_comment: Create Basin. Heavy cast iron construction, shorter and wider than a cauldron with thicker walls — resists pressure well. The open top means internal over-pressure vents upward before the walls breach (VENT mode). Highest elastic yield of all vessel types. Two stages: the first is a resonant metallic groan, the second is structural deformation. The basin's wide base also means compression multiplier is very high — it's practically immune to being crushed from outside.

pressure._note: Wide, thick-walled cast iron basin. Highest elastic yield threshold — a normal brew explosion barely registers. Open top means expansion pressure vents upward (VENT breach) before the walls give. The compression_multiplier reflects the basin's wide stable base: essentially indestructible from external crush. Primary failure mode is internal over-pressure (expansion) from a massively failed brew.
